comment_text.sn34.2:1.4: They cannot stay long, or determine the length of the absorbed state.

root_text.sn34.2:1.4: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ ṭhitikusalo.
root_text.sn34.2:1.5: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ ṭhitikusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo.
root_text.sn34.2:1.6: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī neva samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo hoti, na ca samādhismiṁ ṭhitikusalo.
root_text.sn34.2:1.7: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo ca hoti, samādhismiṁ ṭhitikusalo ca.
root_text.sn34.2:1.8: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo ca hoti samādhismiṁ ṭhitikusalo ca ayaṁ imesaṁ catunnaṁ jhāyīnaṁ aggo ca seṭṭho ca mokkho ca uttamo ca pavaro ca.
root_text.sn34.2:1.9: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, gavā khīraṁ, khīramhā dadhi, dadhimhā navanītaṁ, navanītamhā sappi, sappimhā sappimaṇḍo tatra aggamakkhāyati;
root_text.sn34.2:1.10: evameva kho, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo ca hoti samādhismiṁ ṭhitikusalo ca ayaṁ imesaṁ catunnaṁ jhāyīnaṁ aggo ca seṭṭho ca mokkho ca uttamo ca pavaro cā”ti.

translation_text.sn34.2:1.4: One meditator is skilled in immersion but not in remaining in it.
translation_text.sn34.2:1.5: One meditator is skilled in remaining in immersion but is not skilled in immersion.
translation_text.sn34.2:1.6: One meditator is skilled neither in immersion nor in remaining in it.
translation_text.sn34.2:1.7: One meditator is skilled both in immersion and in remaining in it.
translation_text.sn34.2:1.8: Of these, the meditator skilled in immersion and in remaining in it is the foremost, best, leading, highest, and finest of the four.
translation_text.sn34.2:1.9: From a cow comes milk, from milk comes curds, from curds come butter, from butter comes ghee, and from ghee comes cream of ghee. And the cream of ghee is said to be the best of these.
translation_text.sn34.2:1.10: In the same way, the meditator skilled in immersion and remaining in it is the foremost, best, leading, highest, and finest of the four.”
